sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
greens = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green squares:")
for n in greens:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("\nCenters:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} at {n['boundary']}")

# Check frames
print("\nFrames (c color, pixels=15 or 16):")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] >= 15:
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Wait, the right frame is no longer showing up in the segmentation! Previously there was a frame at [26,44]-[30,48], but now there's only the left frame at [32,11]-[36,15] and the bottom bar at [63,0]-[63,52].
The right green square is at [27,42]-[29,44], and the frame was at [26,44]-[30,48]. When the green square moved into the frame's area, the frame disappeared! This suggests that placing the green square into the frame completes that part of the puzzle.
So the goal is:
1. Move each green square into its corresponding frame
2. The right green square has been placed into the right frame (frame disappeared)
3. Now I need to place the left green square into the left frame
The left frame is at [32,11]-[36,15]. The left green square is at [30,30]-[32,32]. I need to:
1. Click the left green square to make it active (W center)
2. Move it to the left frame position
The left frame is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The green square is 3x3, so it needs to be at approximately [33,12]-[35,14] to fit inside the frame.
Currently the left green square is at [30,30]-[32,32] with c center. I need to:
1. Click it to activate (make W center)
2. Move LEFT to get to col 12-14
3. Move DOWN to get to row 33-35
But wait, earlier RIGHT moved the left green square when it was active. But now the right green is active. Let me first click the left green to activate it, then move it.
Actually, let me reconsider. The right green square was active and RIGHT moved it right. LEFT moved it left. UP moved it up. So arrow keys move the active square in the corresponding direction.
Now I need to activate the left green square and move it to the left frame. Let me click the left green square first.
